item.title: Chile Inflation Expectations Spike to Highest Since March 2023
item.description: Chile’s one-year breakevens — a measure of inflation expectations — jumped to the highest since March 2023 on Friday after the government loosened a fuel stabilization mechanism, triggering a jump in gasoline prices.

item.title: Sixth Street Nears Deal for 80% of Sunderland Women’s Team
item.description: Sixth Street Partners, one of the most active private equity investors in football, is nearing an acquisition of a majority stake in Sunderland AFC’s women’s team, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Shale Oil Drilling Growth Primed to Restart in 2026, Citi Says
item.description: Higher oil prices driven up by the conflict in the Middle East stand to push some of America’s biggest shale producers to start adding drilling rigs in the second half of this year and more than 100,000 barrels per day of increased output by 2027, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: ‘Mispriced’ Warner Bros. Deal Spread Creates Windfall Potential
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp. may have won out against Netflix Inc. in the fight to buy Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., but there are enough market cross-currents and doubts around the pending $111 billion deal to make buying shares of the target an enticing wager.

item.title: Fed’s Barkin Says Inflation Progress May Be At Risk of Stalling
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said the US-Israel war in Iran threatens to add to already elevated inflationary pressures and clouds the economic outlook at a time when the labor market is fragile.

item.title: Cyber Stocks Sink on Report Anthropic AI Model Poses Security Risks
item.description: Cybersecurity stocks slumped on Friday after a Fortune report raised concern that an Anthropic PBC artificial intelligence model being tested may be used by hackers to skirt current cyber defenses.

item.title: Sony Raises PlayStation 5 Prices By Up to $150 Citing Economic Pressures
item.description: Sony Group Corp. raised the prices of its PlayStation 5 video-game console lineup by as much as $150 on Friday, attributing the hike to “continued pressures in the global economic landscape.”

item.title: CVC Is Said to Explore Divestments for Recordati After Takeover
item.description: CVC Capital Partners Plc plans to explore options including potential divestments for Recordati SpA if it succeeds in its €10.9 billion ($12.5 billion) takeover of the Italian drugmaker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ambani’s Jio Mega IPO Is Said to Set Bank Fees in Line With NSE
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. has set investment banking advisory fees for the planned initial public offering of its telecom unit at about 0.65% of the issue size, according to people familiar with the matter, largely in line with those to be paid by National Stock Exchange of India Ltd.

item.title: Treasury Yields Reach Year’s Highs as Iran Stalemate Lifts Oil
item.description: Treasuries fell, lifting benchmark yields to the highest levels this year, as oil prices resumed the advance unleashed by the US war on Iran, which is entering its fifth week.

item.title: Why a Major Clean Energy Firm Chose Brookfield Over Staying Public
item.description: Also: BMO hires new equities leader from CIBC

item.title: Ethiopia Signs $13.1 Billion of Energy, Mining Investment Deals
item.description: Ethiopia has signed $13.1 billion of investment deals spanning renewable energy, manufacturing, real estate, mining and green ammonia, according to the government.

item.title: Carnival Cuts Profit Outlook as Iran War Pushes up Fuel Cost
item.description: Carnival Corp. cut its full-year profit outlook as surging crude prices are driving up fuel costs.

item.title: India to Borrow 8.2 Trillion Rupees in First-Half of Next Fiscal
item.description: India will borrow 8.2 trillion rupees ($86.5 billion) in the first half of the financial year beginning April 1, around half of the amount scheduled for the full 12-month period, the government said in a statement Friday.

item.title: Armenian Banks to Jointly Loan Record $300 Million for AI Center
item.description: Armenian lenders signed a syndicated loan agreement to provide $300 million for the construction of Firebird AI’s data center, High-Tech Industry Minister Mkhitar Hayrapetyan said Friday, calling it the country’s first joint-financing project of that size.

item.title: Angola to Use $500 Million of Eurobond Proceeds for Debt Buyback
item.description: Angola will use a fifth of the funds raised in a eurobond sale this week to buy back debt maturing in 2028, with the remainder to be allocated to government spending, Finance Minister Vera Daves de Sousa said.

item.title: Hg-Backed Visma Said to Delay Planned London IPO to Next Year
item.description: Software firm Visma AS will delay its plans for an initial public offering to next year, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Leveraged Loans Are Outperforming Junk Bonds By Most Since 2023
item.description: US leveraged loans are on track to outperform junk bonds this month by the most in two and a half years, as some borrowers have shifted debt financing to loans while markets are roiled by conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Slips as Inflation Outlook Worsens
item.description: US consumer sentiment slid to a three-month low in March and year-ahead inflation expectations jumped as the war in the Middle East drives up gasoline prices.

item.title: Guggenheim CIO Says Oil Shock Can Drive 10% Selloff in US Stocks
item.description: US stocks could fall as much as 10% if oil prices remain elevated for several months, a scenario that risks derailing the retail-driven “buy-the-dip” dynamic that has supported markets in recent years, according to Guggenheim Partners Investment Management.

item.title: Eldorado Gold Plan to Buy Foran Opposed by Glass Lewis
item.description: A key proxy adviser firm recommended Eldorado Gold Corp. investors vote against its C$3.8 billion ($2.7 billion) bid for Foran Mining Corp., the latest roadblock after Eldorado’s third-largest shareholder called the takeover too expensive.

item.title: Microsoft Rents Data Center Project Developed for Oracle, OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft Corp. will occupy a data center project previously earmarked for Oracle Corp. and OpenAI, scooping up 900 megawatts of capacity after the other two companies walked away from the site.

item.title: BOE Executive Who Fronted Controversial Cash Redesign to Leave
item.description: The Bank of England’s chief cashier Victoria Cleland will leave her position next year, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: UAE Port Ramps Up Hormuz-Dodging Oil Flows After Iranian Strikes
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has been ramping up oil exports from a vital port that lies outside the Strait of Hormuz, after some of the biggest crude loading infrastructure resumed operations following Iranian drone strikes earlier this month.

item.title: Nasdaq 100 Sinks Into Correction as Big Tech Keeps Falling
item.description: The Nasdaq 100 Index fell into a correction territory on Friday amid a deepening slump in the shares of technology giants that have powered the bull market for most of the past three years.

item.title: Amazon’s Rural Delivery Push Slams Into Walmart
item.description: The turf war between the two retail giants is playing out in towns across the country.

item.title: Prosus Said to Mull Selling 10% Delivery Hero Stake to Aspex
item.description: Delivery Hero SE’s biggest shareholder Prosus NV is considering selling a roughly 10% stake to Aspex Management, the delivery food company’s No. 2 holder, people familiar with the matter said, a move that would heap pressure on management to push ahead with asset sales.

item.title: South Africa’s Key Water Supplier Seeks Better Treaty Terms
item.description: Lesotho is seeking to open talks with South Africa over royalties and compensation for a water-sharing deal that accounts for about 10% of its economy.

item.title: Argentina Rating Upgrade Hinges on Reserve Buildup, Fitch Says
item.description: Argentina’s path to a coveted credit rating upgrade hinges on a sustained buildup of foreign-currency reserves, according to Fitch Ratings.

item.title: BYD Posts Worse-Than-Expected Profit Drop Amid EV Price War
item.description: BYD Co. reported a steeper-than-expected slump in fourth-quarter profit as relentless competition and tighter regulation in China ramped up pressure on the world’s biggest electric vehicle maker to reignite stalling momentum.
